understanding.goal_understanding: Place every selectable piece onto goal tiles marked '+' at the same time; if there are multiple goals, all must be occupied simultaneously to trigger WIN and progress through all levels until the final win state.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: The currently selected piece (shown as P) moves one grid cell per MOVE action, with positions expressed in pixel coordinates where each cell is 3 pixels. Movement can be blocked by walls or adjacent objects; the movement_blocked flags are only hints and don’t guarantee failure. Some adjacent objects (e.g., blocks) can be pushable and may slide along when you move into them, while walls/boundaries stop movement. When multiple selectable pieces exist, you switch control via CLICK on the exact pixel coordinates of the target selectable (shown as S when not selected), changing player.id/position to that piece.

history.summary: Turn 1: MOVE_RIGHT | All directions show adjacent wall; test if any move slides/clips through boundary—try moving right first toward the interior gap near the other selectable.
history.reasoning: All directions show adjacent wall; test if any move slides/clips through boundary—try moving right first toward the interior gap near the other selectable.

history.summary: Turn 2: CLICK | We’re boxed in by walls on all four sides; switch to the other selectable to see if it has a path toward the goal area.
history.reasoning: We’re boxed in by walls on all four sides; switch to the other selectable to see if it has a path toward the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 3: MOVE_RIGHT | This selectable appears boxed in by walls on all four sides; test a move anyway to see if any boundary is passable/glitchy.
history.reasoning: This selectable appears boxed in by walls on all four sides; test a move anyway to see if any boundary is passable/glitchy.

history.summary: Turn 4: MOVE_UP | We’re boxed in by walls on all four sides; attempt a move into the wall to test if any boundary is passable/glitchy and potentially escape the enclosure toward the goal area.
history.reasoning: We’re boxed in by walls on all four sides; attempt a move into the wall to test if any boundary is passable/glitchy and potentially escape the enclosure toward the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 5: CLICK | We’re enclosed at [21,21]; to make progress, switch back to the other selectable that still has space to maneuver toward the goal area.
history.reasoning: We’re enclosed at [21,21]; to make progress, switch back to the other selectable that still has space to maneuver toward the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 6: MOVE_RIGHT | Try moving right into the adjacent wall to see if it allows a push/slide behavior (blocked flags are only heuristic).
history.reasoning: Try moving right into the adjacent wall to see if it allows a push/slide behavior (blocked flags are only heuristic).

history.summary: Turn 7: MOVE_RIGHT | All four directions are currently blocked by walls, so try moving right to probe for any nonstandard wall interaction/slide behavior.
history.reasoning: All four directions are currently blocked by walls, so try moving right to probe for any nonstandard wall interaction/slide behavior.

history.summary: Turn 8: MOVE_RIGHT | We’re boxed in on all sides by walls/other selectable; try moving right to test if pushing/swapping is possible with the adjacent selectable.
history.reasoning: We’re boxed in on all sides by walls/other selectable; try moving right to test if pushing/swapping is possible with the adjacent selectable.

history.summary: Turn 9: CLICK | We’re boxed in by walls on all four sides, so we must switch to the other selectable which may have access to the goal area.
history.reasoning: We’re boxed in by walls on all four sides, so we must switch to the other selectable which may have access to the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 10: MOVE_UP | We need the selected piece to occupy the goal tile area; try moving up into the adjacent goal region (blocked hint may still allow overlap since goals are non-collidable).
